concerns.analysis: Page 20 of the source PDF contains several important sections, including paragraphs 46, 47, and 48 of the General Terms, as well as the beginning of the 'Services Terms and Conditions for Provision of Services'. In the JSON output, the content array for page 20 is completely empty due to an extraction error, meaning this entire page of content is missing from the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 20 contains text beginning with '46) Federal Intellectual Property Bankruptcy Protection Act...' and 'Services Terms and Conditions for Provision of Services'.
concerns.output_quote: The 'content' array for page 20 is empty: []

concerns.analysis: The document contains multiple table structure errors. On Page 5, the table headers ('Item', 'Description', etc.) are incorrectly parsed as the second row (Row 1) appearing *after* the first data row ('1', 'Update Cape Fear...'). Additionally, on Page 3, a data row continuing from the table on Page 2 is completely missed as a table structure and is extracted as a series of disconnected paragraphs.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 5 table data containing the row '1 Update Cape Fear...' followed by another table segment
concerns.output_quote: The JSON table cell array on Page 5 places the data row at `row_start: 0` and the header row ('Item', 'Description', 'Part Number', etc.) incorrectly at `row_start: 1`.
concerns.location: Page 5, element index 1 (table) and Page 3, elements 1-5 (paragraphs instead of table)

concerns.analysis: The list hierarchy is flattened in several areas of the document. For instance, on Page 13 under section '3) Personnel', the source document has a nested sub-list where items 'i)', 'ii)', and 'iii)' are indented children of item 'b)'. In the output JSON, these items are extracted at the same sibling level as 'a)' and 'b)', directly under the parent item, which breaks the structural meaning of the list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
b) This Contract shall not prevent Vendor... provided that:
i) Such use does not conflict with the terms...
ii) Such use does not conflict with any procurement law...
concerns.output_quote: The `children` array under the 'Personnel...' list item contains `{"text": "b) This Contract..."}` followed immediately by sibling elements `{"text": "i) Such use does not conflict..."}` and `{"text": "ii)Such use does not conflict..."}` instead of properly nesting them.
concerns.location: Page 13, element index 6 (list), under item 'Personnel: Vendor shall not substitute...'

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (21): PR10689541: 3808 Upgrade to Cape Fear & Neuse River Digital ; https://buyer.ncgov.com/Buyer/render/1CB5X702R20R6[4/15/2011; https://buyer.ncgov.com/Buyer/render/1CB5X702R20R6[4/15/2011
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=21, pages_affected=20/22. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 2 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://eprocurement.nc.gov/; http://eprocurement.nc.gov/Vendor.html
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=2. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 2 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=2. Examples: p6: ed.daughtry@ncdenr.gov; p6: ed.daughtry@ncdenr.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (2 instance(s)): p13→p14: list item ends “… for purposes of convenience in Contract”, continues as paragraph; p14→p15: list item ends “…act. No additional charges to the Agency”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=2. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 7 form(s) with 27 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=7, total_fields=27. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (3 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...rtment of Environment and Natural Resources (NCDEN"; p2: "...turing a stylized black "NC" with an orange swirl,"; p4: "...tylized cursive "NC" with an orange swirl. Text on"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=3, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF frequently uses underlining to emphasize the start of list items or terms (e.g., 'READ, REVIEW AND COMPLY:' on page 11, 'ORDER OF PRECEDENCE:' on page 12). This underline emphasis is completely lost in the JSON output, which renders them as plain text without any HTML or markdown equivalents for underlining.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined text such as 'READ, REVIEW AND COMPLY:' and 'DEFINITIONS:'
concerns.output_quote: "text": "READ, REVIEW AND COMPLY: It shall be the Vendor’s responsibility..."

concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['http://www.doa.nc.gov/hub/', 'http://www.ips.state.nc.us/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 3 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=3. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
